title: The Property Management Ops Leak Audit
subtitle: A 5-minute diagnostic for 50-1,000-door PM firms. Find where your team is actually losing hours — with real numbers, not Likert guesses.

intro.paragraph: You just grabbed the PM Ops Leak Audit. 5 real questions — numbers, tools you actually use, a one-liner about your intake process — and you'll see your monthly leak in dollars, not a vibe score.
intro.point_value: Real inputs: door count, stack, intake narrative — your output is arithmetic on your answers
intro.point_next: Full result shown — score, $ leak, per-category breakdown, and the 3 fixes I'd prioritize

questions.text: How many doors does your team currently manage?
questions.hint: Total under management across all properties — single-family counts as 1.

questions.text: How many people on the operations side of your team?
questions.hint: PMs + leasing + maintenance coordinators + back office. Exclude vendors.

low.headline: Scale is the issue — or lack of
low.text: At under 100 doors with a small team, automation ROI is real but payback is longer. Focus on 2-3 high-leverage wins, not a full stack rebuild.
steps: Pick ONE workflow (intake OR maintenance routing) and automate end-to-end
Skip the dashboard — spreadsheet reports are fine at this scale

mid.headline: You're in the sweet spot for automation
mid.text: 200-500 doors with 5-15 ops staff is where n8n pays back in under 90 days. Every process still being done by hand is a visible leak.
steps: Map your top 3 time-eating workflows on paper
Automate the one with the most manual handoffs first
Ship it in 2 weeks, not 2 months

high.headline: Enterprise-ish scale — automation is non-negotiable
high.text: At 500+ doors, every process not automated is compounding cost. You probably already know the leaks; the question is sequencing.
steps: Audit which workflows still have humans as the API between two systems
Prioritize by hours-per-week leaked, not ease
Budget 20% of ops team time for ongoing automation maintenance

questions.text: Which of these tools live in your maintenance intake stack today?
questions.hint: Check every one that's part of how a request gets from tenant to vendor.

questions.text: In one sentence, how does a new maintenance request reach the right vendor today?
questions.hint: Be honest. 'Tenant emails us, I forward it' is a valid answer.
questions.placeholder: e.g., Tenant emails the property, Maria forwards to the vendor...

low.headline: Intake is where your hours disappear
low.text: Your tool stack + process description both point to manual routing. This is the #1 highest-ROI fix for PM firms under 500 doors — typical recovery is 6-15 hours/week.
steps: Build a tenant intake form (Tally or native PM tool) that writes directly to AppFolio/Buildium
Route to vendor via SMS or Slack based on issue category — n8n does this in 8 nodes
Measure time-to-vendor before and after; target is <5 min

mid.headline: Halfway to automated — finish it
mid.text: You've got the tools but not the pipes. The gap is usually the human-in-the-middle step between tenant message and vendor dispatch.
steps: Identify the 1-2 manual steps remaining in intake
Automate each with n8n + your PM tool's API
Add SLA tracking so you see when routing takes longer than expected

high.headline: Intake is dialed — look upstream
high.text: If intake's automated, the leak has moved — usually to follow-up or vendor accountability. Worth auditing those next.
steps: Measure average tenant-reported issue close time
Add automated vendor SLA reminders at 24 / 48 / 72 hrs
Feed status updates back to tenants without manual work

questions.text: How many hours per month does your team spend preparing owner reports?
questions.hint: Include data pulling, formatting, review, and sending.

computed_outputs.label: Hours lost to manual ops per week

headline_formula: Managing {door_count} doors with {team_size} ops staff, you're leaking roughly {monthly_leak} per month to manual work — {annual_leak} annually. Weakest area: {weakest_category_name} at {weakest_category_score}/100.

cta.headline: Want me to validate your numbers live?
cta.description: 20-min screen-share. I look at your actual PM tool + reporting flow, rerun this scoring with real data, and tell you which fix to attack first. Free, no pitch.
